title: SCX6 backyard suspension test
excerpt: The first pass on the backyard line felt close, but still a little nervous when the truck unloaded off the sharper ledges. I wanted the rear to settle sooner without turning the whole chassis into a wallow.
content: The first pass on the backyard line felt close, but still a little nervous when the truck unloaded off the sharper ledges. I wanted the rear to settle sooner without turning the whole chassis into a wallow.One turn out of the rear preload made the biggest difference right away. The truck still held itself up on the climb, but it stopped hopping as much when the rear axle reached for the next shelf.Moving the battery forward only a little was enough. The nose tracked better on the uphill transition, and I did not pay much for it on the way back down.This is probably the setup I will keep for the next session. The next thing worth checking is whether the front tires need a little more support before I start chasing spring changes again.

title: Calculate Our Range to Target
excerpt: Testing out my range-finder. This is one of my steel targets set up yonder.
content: Testing out my range-finder. This is one of my steel targets set up yonder.

title: Backyard RC Crawling
excerpt: This is my Axial SCX6 Jeep working through the rougher line behind the shed. The current setup feels planted, but I still want more bite off-camber and a little less chassis slap on the drop-offs.
content: This is my Axial SCX6 Jeep working through the rougher line behind the shed. The current setup feels planted, but I still want more bite off-camber and a little less chassis slap on the drop-offs.Notes for the next pass: soften the rear a touch, check the shock preload, and see whether the front tires are folding over too early on the sharper rock edges.

title: Backyard Fire Ring
excerpt: Last weekend I gathered some stones and built a fire pit. I dig it.
content: Last weekend I gathered some stones and built a fire pit. I dig it.

title: First Entry
excerpt: Camp went up fast once the wind dropped. Stove on the tailgate, damp boots by the fire ring, and just enough daylight left to mark the trailhead on the map before supper.
content: Camp went up fast once the wind dropped. Stove on the tailgate, damp boots by the fire ring, and just enough daylight left to mark the trailhead on the map before supper.Nothing dramatic to report, which is usually a good sign. The notes for this stop are simple: tighten the ridge line next time, bring a second flashlight, and come back when the aspens turn.
